title: Applicability predicate language for typed-context entries
description: Under the composition contract (DEC-0007) each typed-context entry (lens view, mandate, monitor output, raw block) carries an applicability declaration: which agent / classifier / context the entry applies to. Today this is implicit in template authoring (`{% if conversation_history %}`), in agent YAML inputSchema, and in monitor classify routing. Under the unifying heuristic applicability becomes a first-class declarative field with a predicate language that the composition layer evaluates at compile-time (or per-turn) to decide what enters the prompt. The predicate language must express: which agent kinds (workflow agents vs classifiers vs query agents); which scopes (user / project / agent); which lifecycle states (open issues only, enacted decisions only); which tags or attributes; logical composition (AND/OR/NOT). Surfaced as gap #38.

evidence.reference: compileAgent's contextBlocks loop today reads each declared block unconditionally; no applicability filter at the compile boundary.
Classify templates use {% if %} guards on Nunjucks vars to express applicability — implicit, per-template, not declarative.
Heuristic-widening pass identifies applicability as the channel by which selective composition resolves which typed contexts enter which agent prompts.

impact: Without an applicability predicate language: composition contract (DEC-0007) cannot specify when each typed-context entry applies; mandate-block applicability_conditions (FGAP-008) and monitor-spec applicability (FGAP-009) lack a shared expression form; lens-view filters (gap #30, collapsed into DEC-0008) lack a runtime evaluator. Each typed-context kind would invent its own ad-hoc applicability surface — the same fragmentation the substrate arc retires.
canonical_vocabulary: declarative applicability predicates; pre-composition filter expressions; typed-context selectivity
proposed_resolution: Specify an applicability predicate mini-language (or adopt an existing one — couples to ADR-0004's expression-language comparison; CEL is a candidate). Predicates live as a typed field on every typed-context entry's schema (mandates, monitor specs, lens views). Composition layer (DEC-0007) evaluates predicates at compile-time against {agent_kind, scope, context_tags, ...}. Predicates fail closed: missing predicate means 'applies always'; failed evaluation means 'does not apply.' Resolution gated on DEC-0007 for evaluation timing semantics.
